# ExpressDefinition.BaseType property

Regardless of nullability and cardinality, provides the underlying type name used in the express definition. For example, for "OPTIONAL IfcLabel" it would return "IfcLabel", for "SET [1:?] OF IfcInteger" it would return "IfcInteger", and for "IfcGloballyUniqueId" it would return "IfcGloballyUniqueId".

```csharp
public string BaseType { get; }
```
